title: Past Lives
overview: Two childhood friends from Seoul reunite in New York decades later and weigh the lives they might have lived.

title: Minari
overview: A Korean American family moves to an Arkansas farm in search of their own American dream.

title: Parasite
overview: A poor family schemes its way into the household of a wealthy Seoul family, with consequences no one sees coming.

title: Burning
overview: A delivery man becomes entangled with an old classmate and a wealthy stranger with an unsettling hobby.

title: Decision to Leave
overview: A detective investigating a mountain death finds himself drawn to the dead man's enigmatic widow.

title: Broker
overview: Two brokers of an unusual trade and a young mother form an unlikely family on a road trip across Korea.

title: The Farewell
overview: A family stages a wedding so everyone can say goodbye to a grandmother who doesn't know she is dying.

title: Columbus
overview: A Korean-born man stranded in Columbus, Indiana strikes up a friendship amid the town's modernist architecture.

title: Drive My Car
overview: A grieving stage director and his quiet chauffeur travel toward an understanding of loss.

title: Perfect Days
overview: A Tokyo toilet cleaner finds quiet contentment in routine, music cassettes, and trees.

title: Aftersun
overview: A woman reflects on a childhood holiday with her young father in a fading Turkish resort.

title: The Banshees of Inisherin
overview: On a remote Irish island, a lifelong friendship ends abruptly, with strange consequences.

title: CODA
overview: The hearing daughter of a deaf fishing family is torn between home and a future in music.

title: Nomadland
overview: After losing everything in the recession, a woman lives as a van-dwelling modern nomad across the American West.

title: Everything Everywhere All at Once
overview: A laundromat owner is swept into a multiversal battle that hinges on taxes, family, and an everything bagel.

title: Jiro Dreams of Sushi
overview: Portrait of an 85-year-old sushi master, his Tokyo restaurant, and a lifetime of disciplined craft.

title: The Biggest Little Farm
overview: A couple spends eight years building a biodiverse farm on depleted California soil.

title: Chef
overview: A chef quits a controlling restaurant job, buys a food truck, and rebuilds his life one Cubano at a time.

title: The Taste of Things
overview: A cook and the gourmet she has worked beside for twenty years compose a life through food in 1885 France.

title: Tampopo
overview: A truck driver helps a widow perfect her ramen shop in a freewheeling Japanese noodle western.

title: Babette's Feast
overview: A French refugee cook spends her lottery winnings preparing one extraordinary meal for a pious Danish village.

title: Little Forest
overview: A young woman returns to her rural hometown and cooks her way through four seasons of memory and recovery.

title: Okja
overview: A girl risks everything to keep a giant company from taking her best friend, a genetically engineered super-pig.

title: Roma
overview: A year in the life of a domestic worker and the family she serves in 1970s Mexico City.

title: First Cow
overview: A cook and a Chinese immigrant build a quiet friendship and a baking business on stolen milk in 1820s Oregon.

title: Shoplifters
overview: A makeshift Tokyo family living on petty theft takes in a neglected little girl.

title: Yi Yi
overview: A Taiwanese businessman, his wife, and their children face upheaval and mortality over several months in Taipei.

title: Portrait of a Lady on Fire
overview: An eighteenth-century French painter and her young subject develop an intense bond while confined to a coastal estate.

title: Phantom Thread
overview: A renowned dressmaker in 1950s London finds his carefully ordered life disrupted by a new muse.

title: Isle of Dogs
overview: When all dogs are exiled from a Japanese city, a boy undertakes a quest to find his lost pet.

title: Tár
overview: The first female conductor of a major German orchestra navigates power, obsession, and a looming reckoning.
